title: Fitur Ketetanggaan dan Autokorelasi Spasial
scope: Spatial structure as signal. Neighborhood features and spatial autocorrelation.
key_concepts: Locations are rarely independent. Tobler's First Law of Geography states that near things are more related than distant things.
Spatial autocorrelation measures how much a variable's value at one location depends on values at neighboring locations.
Neighborhood features explicitly encode this structure. For example, averaging the target variable or specific feature values of the K nearest neighbors.
These features allow non-spatial models (like random forests) to approximate spatial context without specialized spatial architectures.

definitions.meaning: Tingkat kemiripan suatu nilai dengan nilai di lokasi sekitarnya. Autokorelasi positif berarti area yang berdekatan memiliki nilai yang serupa.
Nilai rata-rata berbobot dari suatu variabel di lokasi-lokasi tetangga.

examples: Predicting retail store sales: a feature could be the average sales of all stores in the same postal code or within a 5 km radius (a spatial lag feature).
exclude: Moran's I math, Geary's C deep dive. Keep it focused on feature engineering rather than pure spatial statistics.
relations: Builds on s01 by moving from static location to relationships between locations. Sets up the risk of spatial leakage in s03.
